source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
An underground line has the six stations A, B, C, D, E and F. The train stops at every station. After reaching the end of the line $A$ or $F$ the train continues in the opposite direction. The train conductor starts his journey in station B. His first stop is in station C. In which station will be his $46^{\text {th}}$ stop?
<image1>

image_dependency: requires_image=false indicates the problem is not intended to rely on visual content
text_dominant=true suggests the full reasoning can be done from the text alone
The question describes stations and direction changes entirely in words; no diagram-specific spatial reading is required
Although an image asset exists in the registry, there is no explicit reference to needing it
multi_step: The solver must track a repeating back-and-forth train movement across six stations
The question combines multiple conditions: start at B, first stop at C, stop at every station, and reverse direction at A and F
Finding the 46th stop typically requires sequence simulation or recognizing a cycle/period, which is more than a single direct lookup
The answer is a single station label, but reaching it requires iterative or modular reasoning
verifiability: answer_type is option/choice, so the final response format is precise and constrained
The target answer is a single station letter, making correctness easy to check
The underlying process can be independently verified by enumerating stops or using cycle arithmetic
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and answer_present=true support that the sample is well-formed

decision_reasons: question, answer, and image assets are all present, and the registry passed with no recorded issues
the sample has strong verifiability because the answer is a constrained single-choice station label
the problem appears to require multi-step reasoning through a repeating stop sequence, which supports candidate value
the only flagged risk is that the image may be nonessential, but this does not reduce core semantic recoverability because the text is sufficient
